title: Shaping a new republic
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on the United States after the ratification of the Constitution

assessment_items.question_content:
>“It is . . . . agreed that the western boundary of the United States which separates them from the Spanish colony of Louisiana, is in the middle of the channel or bed of the river Mississippi, from the northern boundary of the said states to the completion of the thirty-first degree of latitude north of the equator. And his Catholic Majesty has likewise agreed that the navigation of the said river, in its whole breadth from its source to the ocean, shall be free only to his subjects and the citizens of the United States. . . .” 

>-Source: *Pinckney’s Treaty*, Article IV, 1795

**Pinckney’s Treaty most directly resulted from which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The US government’s efforts to forge diplomatic initiatives with European powers in North America
The Spanish-American War in which Spain lost significant territorial holdings in North America
Texas gaining its independence and requesting to be annexed by the United States
Southerners moving westward and engaging in conflicts with the Spanish

assessment_items.question_content:
>“It is . . . . agreed that the western boundary of the United States which separates them from the Spanish colony of Louisiana, is in the middle of the channel or bed of the river Mississippi, from the northern boundary of the said states to the completion of the thirty-first degree of latitude north of the equator. And his Catholic Majesty has likewise agreed that the navigation of the said river, in its whole breadth from its source to the ocean, shall be free only to his subjects and the citizens of the United States. . . .” 

>-Source: *Pinckney’s Treaty*, Article IV, 1795

**What was the intended goal for the United States in the treaty above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: secure navigation on the western border
free trade with Spain
gaining ownership of Louisiana and the port of New Orleans
annexing Florida for the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
>“It is . . . . agreed that the western boundary of the United States which separates them from the Spanish colony of Louisiana, is in the middle of the channel or bed of the river Mississippi, from the northern boundary of the said states to the completion of the thirty-first degree of latitude north of the equator. And his Catholic Majesty has likewise agreed that the navigation of the said river, in its whole breadth from its source to the ocean, shall be free only to his subjects and the citizens of the United States. . . .” 

>-Source: *Pinckney’s Treaty*, Article IV, 1795

**The excerpt would be most useful to historians as a source of information about which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how the United States dealt with the presence of Spanish colonies in North America diplomatically
how the United States dealt with the presence of British and Spanish forces in North America militarily
how the United States acquired land from European countries to consolidate control of North America
how the British and Spanish attempted to reclaim land taken by the United States after the Revolutionary War

assessment_items.question_content:
![Map showing approximate locations of Spanish missions in California. There are 22 Spanish Missions shown in the map. The majority of them were created in the late 1700s. Two of them were built in the early 1800s.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3468b9bc617b7646128e36de34e5147a.svg)

**The major pattern on the map best supports which of the following statements about the actions of the Spanish in the 1700 and 1800s?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Spanish expanded their missions in California, leading to new cultural blending between Indigenous communities’ cultures and Spanish culture.
The Spanish government moved away from their goal of converting Indigenous communities to Catholicism and stopped building missions after 1790.
The Spanish missionaries came into constant conflict with Indigenous peoples and were unsuccessful in building new missions after 1790.
The Spanish government began to gain new colonies in Asia and no longer sought to maintain their territories in North America.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“In my opinion it would be both just and proper to declare the treaty with France to be void--but I think it would be more advisable to direct reprisals than to declare war at present, for the public mind does not appear to me to be quite prepared for it. . . . Whenever the mass of our people are convinced that the war would be just, necessary, and unavoidable, they will be content that it should be declared, and will support it vigorously, but I doubt whether that conviction however well founded, is as yet so prevailing and general as it ought to be. . . .”

>-Source: John Jay, letter to William North, 1798

**The excerpt best reflects which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: disagreements over whether to engage in military disputes with European countries
conflicts over engaging in Pinckney’s Treaty with the Spanish
fear over the passage of the Alien and Sedition Acts
increased pressure from the British for support in the Napoleonic Wars

assessment_items.question_content:
>“In my opinion it would be both just and proper to declare the treaty with France to be void--but I think it would be more advisable to direct reprisals than to declare war at present, for the public mind does not appear to me to be quite prepared for it. . . . Whenever the mass of our people are convinced that the war would be just, necessary, and unavoidable, they will be content that it should be declared, and will support it vigorously, but I doubt whether that conviction however well founded, is as yet so prevailing and general as it ought to be. . . .”

>-Source: John Jay, letter to William North, 1798

 **The ideas expressed in the excerpt were most directly motivated by which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: uncertainty about how to respond to increased conflict with the French
devastation left by the Revolutionary War
concerns about the need for mass mobilization in future conflicts
tensions created after entering into a treaty with Britain

assessment_items.question_content:
>“In my opinion it would be both just and proper to declare the treaty with France to be void--but I think it would be more advisable to direct reprisals than to declare war at present, for the public mind does not appear to me to be quite prepared for it. . . . Whenever the mass of our people are convinced that the war would be just, necessary, and unavoidable, they will be content that it should be declared, and will support it vigorously, but I doubt whether that conviction however well founded, is as yet so prevailing and general as it ought to be. . . .”

>-Source: John Jay, letter to William North, 1798

**The ideas expressed by Jay in the excerpt were most similar to those of which of the Founders?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: George Washington
James Madison
Thomas Jefferson
James Monroe

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Art. 1: Henceforth all hostilities shall cease; peace is hereby established, and shall be perpetual; and a friendly intercourse shall take place between the said United States and Indian tribes . . . . 
And in consideration of the peace now established . . . the said Indian tribes do hereby cede and relinquish forever, all their claims to the lands lying eastwardly and southwardly of the general boundary line now described: . . . .”

>-Source: Treaty of Greenville, 1795

**Which of the following most likely motivated Congress to enter into the treaty in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: continued conflicts between Indigenous communities and Americans who sought to encroach on Indigenous land in the Northwest Territory
continued instances of interracial marriages between Indigenous peoples and Americans
a push by frontier settlers to create reservations for Indigenous communities in unwanted territory
a conflict between the French and Americans over boundary lines between the Northwest territory and the Louisiana territory

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Art. 1: Henceforth all hostilities shall cease; peace is hereby established, and shall be perpetual; and a friendly intercourse shall take place between the said United States and Indian tribes . . . . 
And in consideration of the peace now established . . . the said Indian tribes do hereby cede and relinquish forever, all their claims to the lands lying eastwardly and southwardly of the general boundary line now described: . . . .”

>-Source: Treaty of Greenville, 1795

**The treaty expressed in the excerpt contributed most directly to debates in the United States about which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the belief in the right of Americans to continue to expand westward
the response to increased presence of European powers in the Americas
the role of the president in setting foreign policy
the expansion of slavery into unorganized territories

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68768ec63f8a306228f4909754a5350f.jpg)

>**An image of farmers in Western Pennsylvania tarring and feathering a tax collector during the Whiskey Rebellion of 1791.** Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Whiskey_Insurrection.JPG)

**The actions shown in the image depict which of the following trends in 1790s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: resistance to the increased strength of the federal government
rise in support for a constitutional convention
debates over involvement in foreign conflicts, like the French Revolution
resistance to increased corruption in the Treasury department

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68768ec63f8a306228f4909754a5350f.jpg)

>**An image of farmers in Western Pennsylvania tarring and feathering a tax collector during the Whiskey Rebellion of 1791.** Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Whiskey_Insurrection.JPG)

**Which of the following best describes the response made by the US government to the events depicted in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The federal government raised a militia to put down the rebellion.
The federal government raised taxes on the state of Pennsylvania for the rebellion.
The federal government passed the Sedition Act to punish people who criticized the government.
The federal government repealed the tax on whiskey that caused the rebellion.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68768ec63f8a306228f4909754a5350f.jpg)

>**An image of farmers in Western Pennsylvania tarring and feathering a tax collector during the Whiskey Rebellion of 1791.** Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Whiskey_Insurrection.JPG)

**Which of the following was a major change in American politics from the 1780s to the 1790s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: rise of a strong federal government after the ratification of the Constitution
adoption of an antislavery legislation by the federal government
decline in the federal government’s power to tax the American public
rise of political parties defined largely by education level

assessment_items.question_content:
>“It has been much urged that a bank will give great convenience in the collection of taxes . . . yet the Constitution allows only the means which are ‘necessary,’ not those which are merely ‘convenient’ . . . there is not one [power] which ingenuity may torture into convenience, in some instance or other, to someone so long a list of enumerated powers. It would swallow up all the delegated powers, and reduce the whole to one power . . . . Therefore it was that the Constitution restrained them to the necessary means; that is to say, to those means without which the grant of the power would be nugatory (useless).”

>-Source: Thomas Jefferson on the National Bank, 1791

**Which of the following Framers of the Constitution would have been most likely to disagree with Jefferson’s views, as expressed in this excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Alexander Hamilton
James Madison
Patrick Henry
George Mason

assessment_items.question_content:
>“It has been much urged that a bank will give great convenience in the collection of taxes . . . yet the Constitution allows only the means which are ‘necessary,’ not those which are merely ‘convenient’ . . . there is not one [power] which ingenuity may torture into convenience, in some instance or other, to someone so long a list of enumerated powers. It would swallow up all the delegated powers, and reduce the whole to one power . . . . Therefore it was that the Constitution restrained them to the necessary means; that is to say, to those means without which the grant of the power would be nugatory (useless).”

>-Source: Thomas Jefferson on the National Bank, 1791

**Based on the excerpt, which of the following issues of the period most likely concerned Jefferson?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The balance of power between the federal and state governments
The rise of a two-party system
The balance between liberty and order
The peacetime alliances with European countries

assessment_items.question_content:
>“I have already intimated to you the danger of parties . . . with particular reference to . . . geographical discriminations. . ."

>-Source: George Washington, Farewell Address, 1796

**Which of the following events best shows a departure from the ideas expressed in the passage?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: rise of political factions
involvement in the French Revolution
engaging in treaties with Spain and Britain
end of the Atlantic Slave Trade

assessment_items.question_content:
>“I have already intimated to you the danger of parties . . . with particular reference to . . . geographical discriminations. . ."

>-Source: George Washington, Farewell Address, 1796

**In highlighting “geographical discriminations,” Washington was referring most directly to which of the following trends?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: growing sectionalism in the United States over issues like slavery
debates over the balance of power between state and federal governments
conflicts with Indigenous communities over expanding west
environmental impact of unregulated farming in the South

assessment_items.question_content:
> “By interweaving our destiny with that of any part of Europe, [we] entangle our peace and prosperity in the toils of European ambition, rivalship, interest, humor, or caprice. . . . It is our true policy to steer clear of permanent alliances with any portion of the foreign world.” 

>-Source: George Washington, Farewell Address, 1796

**Which of the following developments in the early 1800s best represented the continuation of the ideas expressed in the passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rise of a foreign policy of non-involvement
A constitutional amendment banning political parties
Supporting the French in the French Revolutionary Wars
The creation of the Alien and Sedition Acts

assessment_items.question_content:
> “By interweaving our destiny with that of any part of Europe, [we] entangle our peace and prosperity in the toils of European ambition, rivalship, interest, humor, or caprice. . . . It is our true policy to steer clear of permanent alliances with any portion of the foreign world.” 

>-Source: George Washington, Farewell Address, 1796

**Which of the following issues of the period was Washington most likely concerned within the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: engagement in European conflicts
universal white male suffrage
dependence on an agricultural economy
expansion west into the Ohio River Valley
